ping

Patch Set 6:

Could you kindly please review this patch? Thank you!
Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

Amir, can you please review this patch.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

James, what is the ioctl compatibility requirement here? New tools must be able to work with a few previous Lustre kernel versions? If so, how does a tool compiled with the new structures in this patch work properly with previous Lustre versions where the csum fields don't exist? I must have missed something here.

-1 for now until the question is answered.

Patch Set 6:

The only tool that's affected in this case is lnetctl which was added in 2.7 as part of the DLC project.  I'm not clear on the need for backwards compatibility in this case.

Patch Set 6:

I want to make sure the 2.7 utilities will work with the upcoming 2.8 work. It is quite possible that lnetctl test could be added to auster in 2.8 and I want to avoid any interop issues.
Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 6: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as ee51e8ebc96baf37d59ab771e61f8fc9f8908266

parents.subject: LU-5732 hsm: complementary testsuite for user HSM API

commit.subject: LU-6034 lnet: add default case for check summing
commit.message:
LU-6034 lnet: add default case for check summing

To avoid breaking backwards ioctl compatiability
this patch adds data fields to ioctl data structures
to report the check summing algorithims supported

Change-Id: Idb5fd6633a7f7c2e04bb582f122d625dd95afb25
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13092
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Isaac Huang <he.huang@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
